before_code:
noncomputable instance (P : HeckePair G) : DecidableEq (HeckeCoset P) :=
  Classical.decEq _
after_code:
-- in a proof: `classical` tactic
-- before a specific noncomputable def: `open scoped Classical in`
-- or take `[DecidableEq X]` as an instance argument
description: Never declare a global `noncomputable instance : DecidableEq X := Classical.decEq _` (or any classical Decidable instance). Mathlib maintainer feedback (mathlib4 PR #41251, 2026-07-02): 'A noncomputable decidability instance is precisely as useful as a chocolate teapot.' Instead: use the `classical` tactic inside proofs; `open scoped Classical in` immediately before a specific noncomputable def; or add `[DecidableEq X]` as an instance argument. A global classical instance can also shadow genuine decidable instances downstream.

before_code:
({(h : G) * g} : Set G) * (H : Set G) = {(h' : G) * g} * (H : Set G)  -- coset equality as sets
lemma set_singleton_mul_left_cancel ... -- hand-rolled cancellation wrappers
after_code: (QuotientGroup.mk (h * g) : G ⧸ H) = QuotientGroup.mk (h' * g)  -- use QuotientGroup.eq, mk_out_eq_mul, leftCoset_eq_iff
description: Do not express left-coset equality as singleton set products `{x} * (H : Set G) = {y} * H`; state it in `G ⧸ H` via `QuotientGroup.mk x = QuotientGroup.mk y` (API: QuotientGroup.eq, mk_out_eq_mul) or, when sets are genuinely needed, as `x • (H : Set G) = y • H` (API: leftCoset_eq_iff). The singleton-product form forces hand-rolled cancellation lemmas that duplicate mathlib.

before_code: noncomputable def m (g₁ g₂ : P.Δ) : (HeckeCoset P) →₀ ℤ := ...
after_code: noncomputable def structureConstants ... -- descriptive lowerCamelCase name
description: Public defs/lemmas must have descriptive names; never single letters (e.g. `def m` for Hecke structure constants). Single-letter names are undiscoverable in search/autocomplete and will be flagged in mathlib review.
